facade_colour: warm red brick with buff/cream polychromatic accents; bargeboard trim in gable

hcd_data.architectural_style: House-form, Row, Bay-and-Gable (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a row of three, the properties retain most of their original building features including their mirrored facades, cross-gabled roofline, brick cladding with polychromatic brick detailing, first storey bay windows, and wooden bargeboards. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red brick with buff/cream polychromatic accents and bargeboard trim visible in gable
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Well-maintained brick facade; polychromatic details largely intact
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor: 2 windows in bay projection. Second floor: 2 windows with decorative brick voussoirs. Partially obscured by tree and hedge.
photo_observations.door_details: Entrance beside bay window; covered entry
photo_observations.cornice_details: Decorative cornice consistent with existing params; bargeboard in gable
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Three-sided projecting bay spanning ground and first floors; part of row of three
photo_observations.porch_details: Low stoop with wooden steps visible through vegetation.
photo_observations.chimney_details: Single chimney visible at roof ridge.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Polychromatic brick detailing; decorative bargeboard in gable; wooden porch elements
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Minimal visible alterations; retains Bay-and-Gable form as part of row of three
photo_observations.notes: Row Bay-and-Gable Victorian, part of row of three (108-110-112).

notes: Row Bay-and-Gable Victorian, part of row of three (108-110-112).

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, row, bay-and-gable typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include cornice, decorative brick, original windows, bay window, string courses.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a row of three, the properties retain most of their original building features including their mirrored facades, cross-gabled roofline, brick cladding with polychromatic brick detailing, first storey bay windows, and wooden bargeboards. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Row two-storey bay-and-gable in warm red brick with buff/cream polychromatic accents; bargeboard trim in gable under a cross-gable roof. Features include a double-height three-sided projecting bay, decorative bargeboards, string courses, a decorative cornice and polychromatic brickwork. Minimal visible alterations; retains Bay-and-Gable form as part of row of three. Pre-1889 construction.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a row of three, the properties retain most of their original building features including their mirrored facades, cross-gabled roofline, brick cladding with polychromatic brick detailing, first storey bay windows, and wooden bargeboards. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
